component.name: Allow2 Parental Freedom
component.summary: Smart parental freedom for Linux desktops and Steam Deck

description.p: Allow2 brings smart, activity-aware parental freedom to Linux. Monitor and manage screen time, gaming, internet, and social media with per-activity quotas, progressive warnings, and request-more-time — all from the Allow2 app on your phone.

Unlike other parental control apps that only let you set limits by day of week, Allow2 uses Day Types — School Day, Weekend, Holiday, Sick Day, Birthday — so limits match how your family actually lives.

ul.li: Per-activity quotas: separate limits for Gaming, Internet, Social Media, and total Screen Time
Cross-device tracking: gaming time on the PC counts against the same daily limit as the console or phone
Request More Time: children request extra time from the lock screen; parents approve from their phone
Offline support: cached permissions with grace period — no lockout if WiFi drops
Steam Deck: works in both Game Mode (gamescope overlay) and Desktop Mode (SDL2 fullscreen overlay)
Process monitoring: automatically detects what your child is doing by scanning running applications
Parent Feedback Channel: report issues directly to Allow2 from the device

description.p: Works on Steam Deck, Ubuntu, Fedora, Arch, and any Linux distribution. Parents manage everything from the free Allow2 app (iOS and Android).
Initial alpha release with core features:

ul.li: Device pairing via 6-digit PIN or QR code
Child identification with OS user mapping or interactive selector
Request More Time from lock screen
SDL2 overlay for Desktop Mode, Steam browser overlay for Game Mode
Process classification for automatic activity detection
